Source: Vertex AI Documentation
URL: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs
Fetched: 2026-03-22

# Vertex AI Documentation Overview

## Core Description
"Vertex AI is a machine learning (ML) platform that lets you train and deploy ML models and AI applications."

## Key Products & Features

**Generative AI Components:**
- Gemini: "Google's multimodal generative AI model, to process images, videos, and text"
- Model Garden for discovering and deploying generative AI models
- Text and multimodal embeddings services
- Imagen for image generation
- Extensions and Studio interfaces

**Training Solutions:**
- AutoML for tabular, image, and forecasting tasks
- Vertex AI serverless training
- Vertex AI training clusters
- Ray on Vertex AI for distributed training
- Neural Architecture Search
- Vertex AI Vizier for optimization
- Deep Learning Containers and VM Images

**MLOps & Management:**
- Vertex AI Pipelines for workflow orchestration
- Model Registry for version management
- Feature Store for ML feature serving
- Experiments and TensorBoard for tracking
- Model Monitoring and Evaluation services
- Explainable AI for model interpretation

**Inference:**
- Online and batch prediction endpoints
- Vector Search capabilities
- Support for custom and prebuilt containers
- TPU and GPU acceleration options

## Development Tools
- Colab Enterprise
- Vertex AI Workbench (managed, user-managed, and instances)
- Jupyter notebook support
- Python SDK and APIs

## Notable Deprecations
AutoML Text has been superseded: "Migrate from AutoML text to Gemini"

## Additional Capabilities
Vector Search 2.0 for intelligent retrieval, model evaluation for fairness, hyperparameter tuning, and comprehensive monitoring dashboards.

**Last Updated:** February 5, 2026
